# PyAgent — Master Agent Log
_Endpoint: PUT /api/agent-log_
This file is written by the `/api/agent-log` backend endpoint and serves as
the live activity log for the master orchestration agent. The OrchestrationGraph
UI component reads this file to display agent activity in real time.
[00:10:00] User: ok, lets start the next project

[00:45:00] User: yes please make these improvements and reduce ci.yml work
[00:45:00] @0master: Implemented CI workload reduction by introducing a single governance gate job for pre-commit + strict mypy, removing duplicated per-shard mypy execution, conditionally skipping Rust builds on lightweight shards, and adding CI workflow contract tests to enforce the new structure.
[08:15:00] User: continue
[08:15:00] @0master: Closed post-merge governance for prj0000111 by registering it in kanban.json/kanban.md and data/projects.json, advanced data/nextproject.md to prj0000112, and validated consistency with project_registry_governance.py (VALIDATION_OK: projects=111, kanban_rows=111).
[09:10:00] User: yes make an @10idea agent that monitors ideas and merges similar projects into a new one while archiving old ideas.
[09:10:00] @0master: Added @10idea as a first-class agent (.agent/.tools/.skills/memory/log files), wired it into CodeBuilder/OrchestrationGraph/ProjectManager and dev docs API allowlist, and documented merge-then-archive governance behavior for docs/project/ideas.
[11:45:00] User: I wish the agents to be working more independent and where possible, in parallel.
[11:45:00] @0master: Shifted coordination to a parallel-first policy by adding independent work-package rules, explicit file-ownership boundaries, and mandatory synchronization barriers before implementation and before validation/git while keeping all git-affecting operations strictly sequential.
[12:20:00] User: yes improve more
[12:20:00] @0master: Rolled the parallel policy into specialist agents @1project through @10idea with role-specific independence contracts and convergence gates, then validated governance docs tests successfully (17 passed).
[13:00:00] User: I think @0master needs to track all the parallel work of itself and all the sub agents with a parallel agent register that can be shared with other agents especially the files they touch and lockfiles etc , i think this is best done in json format in .github\agents\data\parallel_agents_register.json
[13:00:00] @0master: Created the shared parallel agent register JSON at .github/agents/data/parallel_agents_register.json and updated master/governance policy so parallel waves must record package ownership, touched files, lock ids, and lock acquisition/release before downstream handoff.
[13:35:00] User: do a next step that makes this fully executable by adding a small script (for example scripts/parallel_register.py) with commands like acquire-lock, release-lock, touch-file, and close-wave so updates are atomic and standardized across agents.
[13:35:00] @0master: Added scripts/parallel_register.py with atomic file-lock based register updates and the requested commands (acquire-lock, release-lock, touch-file, close-wave), added tests/test_parallel_register.py, validated with 4 passing tests, and documented command usage in shared governance/tools guidance.
